plan: 1. Enhance the existing footer in `src/layout/shell.ts` — replace the single-line footer text with structured trust statements: 'No cookies', 'No analytics', 'No backend profiling', 'All analysis runs locally'. Use a `<ul>` or series of `<span>` elements inside the existing `<footer>` so each statement is individually identifiable (e.g., with data attributes or semantic markup) for both styling and testability.

2. Update `src/layout/shell.css` — style the privacy footer items as an inline horizontal list using flexbox with dot/pipe separators. Use existing design tokens (`--color-text-muted`, `--font-size-xs`, `--spacing-sm`, `--spacing-md`). Add a subtle shield or lock icon via CSS pseudo-element or inline SVG for credibility. Ensure the footer remains visible at all viewport widths with wrapping on mobile.

3. Write unit tests in `tests/us-031/privacy-footer.test.ts` — follow existing test patterns (DOM rendering + CSS file reading). Verify: (a) footer element exists, (b) contains text 'No cookies', (c) contains text 'No analytics', (d) contains text 'No backend profiling', (e) contains text matching 'analysis runs locally', (f) footer is a persistent page element (always rendered). Optionally verify footer CSS uses design tokens.
4. Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to confirm all checks pass, including regression on US-004 and US-005 tests.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: Render shell and assert footer contains all four required trust statements (no cookies, no analytics, no backend profiling, all analysis runs locally)
Unit test: Verify footer uses semantic HTML (`<footer>` tag) and is the last child of the shell
CSS verification: Read shell.css and confirm footer styles use design tokens from tokens.css
Regression: Ensure existing US-004 responsive shell tests still pass (footer element exists, DOM order preserved)
Regression: Ensure existing US-005 theme token tests still pass
Manual: Verify footer is visible on mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1200px+) viewports
Manual: Confirm footer text contrast meets WCAG AA requirements against background
risks: Existing US-004 tests assert specific footer content ('All analysis runs locally. No data leaves your browser.') — changing the footer text may break these tests and require updating them
Footer wrapping on very narrow viewports (< 320px) could look cluttered with four statements — need to verify mobile layout
